# DroppedNeedle Contributor License Agreement

**Version 1.0**

Thank you for contributing to DroppedNeedle.

This agreement sets out the terms under which you contribute code, documentation, or other material to the DroppedNeedle project. It exists so that the project has a clear, unambiguous right to use and distribute what you contribute, including under licence terms other than the AGPL-3.0 that the public repository uses.

**You keep the copyright in your work.** This is a licence, not an assignment. You are free to use, sell, or relicense your own contribution however you like, anywhere else. What you are granting is a licence to the project alongside the rights you already hold.

By signing, you accept the terms below for your past and future contributions to this project.

---

## 1. Definitions

**"You"** means the individual, or the legal entity, that signs this agreement.

**"The Project Owner"** means Harvey Bragg, the copyright holder of DroppedNeedle, and any successor to whom ownership of the project is transferred.

**"Contribution"** means any original work of authorship that you intentionally submit to the project, in any form, including code, documentation, configuration, tests, and translations. It includes work you have already submitted before signing this agreement.

**"Submit"** means any form of communication sent to the project or its maintainers for the purpose of inclusion in the project. This includes pull requests, patches, issues, and messages on the project's communication channels. It excludes anything you clearly mark in writing as "Not a Contribution."

## 2. Copyright licence

You grant the Project Owner a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, no-charge, royalty-free, irrevocable copyright licence to reproduce, prepare derivative works of, publicly display, publicly perform, sublicense, and distribute your Contribution and any derivative works of it.

**This licence expressly includes the right to license your Contribution, and works derived from it, under any licence terms the Project Owner chooses, including licence terms that are not open source.** This is what allows DroppedNeedle to be offered both under the AGPL-3.0 and under separate commercial terms.

You reserve all right, title, and interest in and to your Contributions. Nothing in this agreement transfers ownership.

## 3. Patent licence

You grant the Project Owner and every recipient of software distributed by the project a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, no-charge, royalty-free, irrevocable (except as stated in this section) patent licence to make, have made, use, offer to sell, sell, import, and otherwise transfer your Contribution. This licence covers only those patent claims you own or control that are necessarily infringed by your Contribution alone, or by the combination of your Contribution with the project.

If any entity brings patent litigation alleging that the project, or a Contribution within it, infringes a patent, any patent licence granted under this agreement to that entity terminates as of the date the litigation is filed.

## 4. Your representations

You represent that:

1. You are legally entitled to grant the licences above.
2. Each Contribution is your original creation, or you have identified in the Contribution any part that is not, along with its source and licence.
3. If your employer has rights to intellectual property you create, you have either received permission to make the Contribution on their behalf, or your employer has waived those rights, or your employer has signed this agreement.
4. The GitHub account you sign from is yours, and the name and email address on your commits identify you. Anonymous and pseudonymous contributions cannot be accepted: a licence granted by someone who cannot be identified is a licence that cannot be relied on.
5. You will notify the Project Owner if any of the above becomes inaccurate.

If you are contributing on behalf of a company rather than as an individual, contact the Project Owner before opening a pull request. A separate corporate agreement is required.

## 5. No warranty, no obligation

Your Contribution is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind, express or implied.

The Project Owner is under no obligation to use, merge, or keep any Contribution.

## 6. Governing law

This agreement is governed by the law of England and Wales.

---

## How to sign

Open a pull request. A bot will comment asking you to sign, and you sign by replying to the pull request with exactly:

```
I have read the CLA Document and I hereby sign the CLA
```

Your signature is recorded against your GitHub account, once, and applies to all your future contributions. You will not be asked again.

Your public comment on the pull request is itself the record of your assent, alongside the signature file the bot maintains.

If you have already contributed before this agreement existed, you will be asked to sign on your next pull request, and your signature will cover your earlier contributions too.

---

*Questions about this agreement can be raised as a GitHub issue or on the project Discord.*
